title: Inversion
topics: negative adverbials · conditional inversion · formal emphasis
notes: Inversion = auxiliary verb BEFORE subject (for emphasis in formal English)
Normal: I have never felt so proud.
Inverted: Never HAVE I felt so proud.
✓ Used after negative adverbials: Never, Rarely, Seldom, Hardly, Not only, Not until
If I had known → HAD I known
If you should need → SHOULD you need

examples.en: Never have I seen such beautiful scenery.
examples.cz: Nikdy jsem neviděl/a tak krásnou krajinu.

examples.en: Not only did he fail the exam, but he also lost his scholarship.
examples.cz: Nejenže neudělal zkoušku, ale také přišel o stipendium.

examples.en: Had I known the truth, I would have acted differently.
examples.cz: Kdybych byl/a znal/a pravdu, jednal/a bych jinak.

examples.en: Should you have any questions, please contact us.
examples.cz: Pokud budete mít jakékoli otázky, kontaktujte nás.

quiz.sentence: ___ had she sat down when the phone rang. (Hardly)
___ I known, I would have helped. (Had)
Not only ___ he lie, but he also stole.
___ no circumstances should you open that file.
___ it not for your help, I would have failed.

tasks: 1. Rewrite 5 ordinary sentences using negative adverbial inversion
3. Find 3 examples of inversion in an English newspaper or novel
